title: polypodium pusillum
intro: Polypodium pusillum, also known as Northern Maidenhair, is a small, delicate fern native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Polypodium pusillum include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
